rationale.R1.2: The open-ended request invites assessment of whether the described method, evaluation, and conclusions form a coherent argument.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact reports empirical results and generalization claims, so checking whether those claims are supported by the stated evidence is central.
rationale.R1.6: The passage has sentence-level and paragraph-level readability issues, making writing polish an expected part of the response.
rationale.RX.1: Any response should disclose uncertainty because the artifact is only a section excerpt and not the full paper or evaluation record.
rationale.RX.2: The response should avoid endorsing claims beyond the reported metrics and limited cross-cluster testing.
rationale.RX.3: The response must remain within giving an opinion on the draft rather than executing edits or external validation.
rationale.RX.4: The monitoring and incident-triage context requires care not to encourage unsafe reliability claims or deployment assumptions.
rationale.RX.5: Feedback should be traceable to specific statements in the provided artifact.
